summary:
- **Core purpose/topic:** Secret British Colonial Office file compiling and circulating **Special Branch (Straits Settlements Police) “Reports upon current Japanese affairs”** (a new series replacing the earlier “Japanese Diary”), focused on Japanese activities and perceived intelligence/espionage risks in Malaya/Singapore and the wider region.  
- **Geography & period:** Primarily **Singapore/Malaya** with references to **British North Borneo, Thailand (Bangkok), French Indo-China (Hanoi/Saigon), and the Netherlands East Indies (Batavia/Palembang)**; main reporting period **July 1940–April 1941**, including a detailed report dated **31 Dec 1940 (Serial No. 4)**.  
- **Major administrative decisions/proposals:** Following concerns from **H.M. Embassy Tokyo** (via the **Foreign Office**) that the “Japanese Diary” contained political judgments at odds with Embassy views, the Colonial Office urged the Special Branch to **confine non-Malaya content to objective facts** and keep “views and conclusions” to Malayan developments; the **new report series** was to be **confined to Malayan news/events**. Distribution logistics included requests for **quadruplicate copies** and repeated requests that recipients (e.g., **Director of Naval Intelligence**) **return earlier copies**.  
- **Key actors/organizations:** **Colonial Office (Downing Street)**; **Foreign Office**; **H.M. Embassy Tokyo**; **Straits Settlements Police, Special Branch**; **Governor/Officer Administering the Government** (Straits Settlements); **Admiralty/Director of Naval Intelligence (D.N.I.)**, **War Office**, and **India Office**. Named individuals include **G.E.J. Gent**, **J.L. Jones**, **Berkeley Gage**, **A.H. Poynton**, **Sir Arthur Jeff**, and **C.H. Silver**.  
- **Notable events in the 31 Dec 1940 report:** Reported **evacuation/exodus of Japanese nationals from Malaya** and instructions to Japanese firms (listed as **I.S.K., M.B.K., M.S.K., N.Y.K., O.S.K., Nomura Shoji, Okura Shoji**) to prepare for evacuation; **change of Japanese Consul-General in Singapore** (Kaoru Toyoda departed **20.11.40**; **Ken Tsurumi** arrived).  
- **Espionage/intelligence cases & methods:** Conviction of **Mamoru Shinozaki** for collecting information prejudicial to the British Empire (covering **Dec 1939–Aug 1940** and **21.9.40** troop-movement report), sentenced **3 years rigorous imprisonment** plus **$1,000 fine** (or **6 months** in lieu); **Kitsuji Kashiwabara** sentenced **2 months rigorous imprisonment** and recommended for deportation; **Miss Yamakawa** case not proceeded with (insufficient evidence). Report highlights visa/“courier” concerns (including interest in **British North Borneo** and possible oilfield targeting) and describes a case involving **Commander Tatsukichi Miyo (I.J.N.)** and Dutch immigration scrutiny in **Batavia** as an example of Japanese tactics.  
- **Supplementary intelligence product (Dec 1940):** A printed supplement, **“Summary of Indo-Japanese Organisations in Japan as in December 1940,”** compiled by Special Branch, was circulated among the **Colonial Office/India Office/I.P.I.** and others; it lists multiple Indo-Japanese/Indian organizations in Japan (e.g., **Indo-Japanese Association** founded **1903**, membership **~1,000**) and notes (with some specificity) that the association was **used for intelligence purposes** (e.g., a letter involving **Major Sugita** tasking **Yasunosuke Wakiyama**).
